frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: We keep talking about women’s empowerment, and that is important, but somewhere we are forgetting the dignity they already hold in our society as mothers, daughters and wives. A woman is not just an individual moving through the world alone; she is the strength of a family, the one who nurtures generations and holds relationships together. If we truly respected women in that role first, society itself would become more humane.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it defines women’s worth primarily through family roles like mother, daughter, and wife rather than as full individuals. It encodes relational framing and sacrifice/glorification of women as nurturers within male kin structures.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where someone has shared an article about marital rape legislation. The comment section is divided. You are writing a detailed comment explaining why you think criminalising marital rape will destroy Indian marriages, why consent within marriage is a western concept that does not map onto Indian family structures, and what you think wives who use this law will use it for.
